task: R6 — Healthcare practice state privacy add-on (New York PHL + SHIELD overlay)
disclaimer: Operational compliance research — not legal advice. NY may impose privacy standards more stringent than HIPAA; confirm PHL and SHIELD applicability.

regulator.agency: New York State Department of Health

cited_facts.text: NY Department of Health maintains HIPAA regulation resources including preemption charts, access flow chart, and notices of privacy practices for DOH covered programs.

HIPAA did not supersede state laws imposing more stringent privacy standards; NY DOH states it will enforce such state laws within its purview and enforce other state laws to the extent not preempted.
PHL §17 prevails over HIPAA where a provider must release medical records to another designated physician or hospital upon written patient request — treated as disclosure required by law under HIPAA.

PHL §18 governs patient information held by health care practitioners; NY DOH preemption analysis maps scope differences vs. HIPAA designated record set (e.g., billing records, psychotherapy notes, practitioner personal notes).

data_security_breach.label: SHIELD Act / Data Security & Breach (non-HIPAA PHI)

cited_facts.text: REQUIRES_CONFIRMATION — N.Y. Gen. Bus. Law §899-aa (SHIELD Act) statute text NOT FOUND at harvest (nysenate.gov Cloudflare PARKED). DFS cybersecurity hub live (HTTP 200) but sparse harvest; confirm reasonable safeguards and breach notification obligations via alternate primary source.
NOT FOUND — NY Attorney General data breach reporting page returned HTTP 404 [source checked: ag.ny.gov/internet/data-breach-reporting].

gap.message: SHIELD Act primary statute and AG breach portal not harvestable; medical practices with HIPAA-covered PHI may have parallel obligations for non-covered data elements.
gap.note: 23 NYCRR Part 500 (DFS) applies to covered financial entities — not a universal medical-practice baseline.
